🔐 Private index URL — recon hardening

chore/private-index-url-secret · MEDIUM-2 from the public-repo exposure audit. The password was always a secret — this hides the rest of the URL.

Before — the public workflow disclosed the whole shape

--extra-index-url "https://ci:${YG_PYPI_PASSWORD}@pypi.yonyon.ai/simple/"
ElementValueIn public repo?
password${YG_PYPI_PASSWORD}no — already a secret ✅
hostpypi.yonyon.aiyes — private index host
usernameciyes — basic-auth user
schemehttp basicyes — auth model

After — the whole URL lives in one secret

env:
  YG_PYPI_INDEX_URL: ${{ secrets.YG_PYPI_INDEX_URL }}   # = https://ci:<pw>@pypi.yonyon.ai/simple/
run: |
  --extra-index-url "${YG_PYPI_INDEX_URL}"
ElementIn public repo?
host / username / scheme / passwordno — all four now server-side in one secret ✅

What didn't change

✓ the self-skip guard (forks without the secret still succeed with WARN)
✓ the install behavior (same package, same index, same result)
→ one new secret to create; the old YG_PYPI_PASSWORD becomes unused
Follow-up to #2814 (op:// genericization). No secret value was ever exposed — this drops the URL shape that maps the private registry.
